Patch Set 1:

Is this new return style also used by the upstream kernel, or is it only for this SLES kernel?  If all newer kernels return the error via ERR_PTR(bh) then it makes more sense to change the osd-ldiskfs code to expect this and  fix it in the wrapper function for older kernels. That would allow removing the wrapper function in the future.

Patch Set 1:

This patch is not only for sles12, but also for rhel7. So we need to land it before b2_8 released.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

> Is this new return style also used by the upstream kernel, or is it only for this SLES kernel? If all newer kernels return the error via ERR_PTR(bh) then it makes more sense to change the osd-ldiskfs code to expect this and fix it in the wrapper function for older kernels. That would allow removing the wrapper function in the future.

Currently, both rhel7 and sles12 use such format function interface. I am not sure whether it will be the standard in the further. But up to the latest stable kernel (linux-4.3), it keeps such style. So I will adjust osd-ldiskfs callers style to match that.

Patch Set 1:

On the other hand, since this patch is not only for sles12, it will not depends on other sles12 patches, instead, it will be on master directly.

parents.subject: LU-7376 tests: sanity-hsm/59 should skip old servers.

commit.subject: LU-7343 osd-ldiskfs: handle ldiskfs_append failure
commit.message:
LU-7343 osd-ldiskfs: handle ldiskfs_append failure

In new linux kernel (linux-3.1x, x>=0), the ldiskfs exported
function ldiskfs_append() return error# via the return value,
instead of via the output parameter @err as it does on other
kernels (linux-2.6). Under such case, the caller should not
assume non-NULL returned value is valid buffer head, it can
stands for error#. So check that properly.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I4dca43bcfd31aafd999f54934a51d258071dab22
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17148
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Yang Sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
